description: Without per-tenant limits, one tenant's heavy load consumes shared capacity and degrades service for every other tenant.
body: Failure mode: a tenant issues expensive queries or high request volume against a shared pool with no quota, starving connections or compute for others. Detection signals: latency rises for many tenants simultaneously; per-tenant metrics show one tenant dominating usage; the pool saturates during one tenant's activity. Do not run a shared pool without per-tenant fairness. Safer fallback: set per-tenant rate limits and resource quotas, apply fair scheduling, monitor per-tenant consumption, and promote a persistently heavy tenant to dedicated capacity rather than letting it degrade the pool.
